key_themes: Record ETF inflows and strong organic base fee growth
Significant Q1 2026 earnings and revenue beat
Expansion in private markets, performance fees, and Aladdin technology
summary: BlackRock delivered exceptional Q1 2026 results announced April 14, with adjusted EPS of $12.53 (beating estimates), revenue of $6.7B (+27% YoY), $130B in total net inflows including a record $132B into iShares ETFs, and 31% growth in adjusted operating income.[[1]](https://www.wsj.com/finance/investing/blackrock-blk-q1-earnings-report-2026-91afa0dc)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/14/blackrock-blk-earnings-q1-2026.html)[[3]](https://www.blackrock.com/corporate/newsroom/press-releases/article/corporate-one/press-releases/blackrock-reports-first-quarter-2026) Despite slight QoQ AUM decline to $13.89T amid broader market volatility, year-over-year growth was robust and performance fees surged. Shares rose 2.5-4% post-earnings, reflecting strong investor confidence in BlackRock’s ETF dominance, private markets pivot, and “Super-Manager” positioning.[[1]](https://www.wsj.com/finance/investing/blackrock-blk-q1-earnings-report-2026-91afa0dc)[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/blackrock-stock-gains-q1-earnings-111700371.html)
sources: 15+ news articles (WSJ, CNBC, Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, BlackRock IR on Q1 2026 earnings), 20+ recent X posts discussing earnings, ETF inflows, and stock reaction (mostly April 2026)
